Aedrian Rimorin Sagap is a software engineer based in Caba, La Union, Philippines. His email is sagapaedrian@gmail.com and his GitHub is github.com/aedriansagap.
Aedrian graduated Magna Cum Laude with a BS in Computer Science from Don Mariano Marcos Memorial State University in Agoo, La Union in May 2026.
Aedrian is a PhilNITS IT Passport (IP) Certified Professional.
Aedrian's programming languages include Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, C, Java, C#, SQL, HTML, and CSS.
Aedrian's frameworks include React Native, React.js, Node.js, and Flask.
Aedrian has database experience with PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Supabase, and ChromaDB.
Aedrian has experience with testing and infrastructure tools including Playwright, k6, Docker, Linux, Git, GitHub, and llama.cpp.
From May 2025 to July 2025, Aedrian worked as an Information Technology Intern at the Department of Science and Technology (DOST) Regional Office I.
During his internship at DOST, Aedrian led an internship development team to build E-sumite, a financial reporting system for the Accounting Office.
At DOST, Aedrian designed a multi-tenant reporting architecture across Regional Office I and its provincial offices, and deployed production-ready code with the ITSM Unit.
The E-sumite system Aedrian built at DOST was permanently adopted and rebranded as FTRACS (Financial Tracking Reports and Compliance System).
Aedrian's undergraduate thesis was VisitTrack, a LAN-Based Visitor Monitoring System, which he led and developed.
For VisitTrack, Aedrian built and containerized a secure visitor monitoring system for the BJMP Agoo District Jail Office using Docker and engineered a low latency LAN-based architecture.
In VisitTrack, Aedrian implemented end-to-end automated testing via Playwright and used k6 to simulate high-volume traffic for server stress-testing.
Aedrian developed RECLAIM, a Multi-Platform Lost & Found System, as an academic project where he served as Leader and Developer.
RECLAIM comprises a React Native mobile application, a responsive web version hosted on Netlify, and a dedicated admin monitoring dashboard hosted on Vercel.
For RECLAIM, Aedrian configured authentication and secure data access using Supabase and PostgreSQL RLS, and implemented real-time updates, multi-image uploads, and offline caching.
Aedrian developed a Local AI Chatbot & RAG Pipeline personal project leveraging Flask and llama.cpp for local AI inference.
Aedrian's Local AI Chatbot integrates ChromaDB to create a data pipeline to generate and store embeddings, implementing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to reduce hallucinations.
Aedrian served as the Club Secretary for the Computer Science Club (CSC) at DMMMSU-SLUC during S.Y. 2024 - 2025.

In December 2025, Aedrian completed an orientation on the Community Based Monitoring System (CBMS) principles and data-driven governance frameworks sponsored by the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA).
